themes.exampleQuote: One of the widest tea selections I have ever seen
a wonderful pseudo-'third space' where you can sip tea, relax, play a board game, study, and talk to other patrons
Staff is extremely friendly and knowledgeable about tea
It's hard to get a seat sometimes because it's crowded
The babaganoush is delicious — I have been back and tried some food

notable_quote: One of my favorite places on Earth — a wonderfully large selection of teas from around the world, served in an intimate and quiet setting.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the breadth of the tea menu, the unhurried atmosphere, and staff who can actually talk about what they're serving — the only friction that surfaces is seating scarcity during busy hours, which reads more as a demand problem than a quality one.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight Pittsburgh creators filmed Dobrá Tea this quarter, generating over 2.6 million TikTok plays — and Dobrá posted once to Instagram in the past thirty days. The city is building a visual record of this place; the place is not adding a single frame to its own. That gap is the defining tension of Dobrá's Spring 2026: review sentiment is among the strongest in the Pittsburgh Cafes family, the conversion path is fully intact, and TikTok creators are doing the discovery work without any coordination from the business. The family leader this issue, Allegheny Coffee & Tea Exchange, holds its position on social momentum — the exact axis where Dobrá, currently ranked sixth in Pittsburgh Cafes, has the most room to move.
tldr_read: Review sentiment that leads the tea category by a wide margin, Instagram that has gone nearly silent, and a TikTok audience the business didn't build and isn't capturing — Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Tea Houses.
tldr_meaning: The review floor is solid and the discovery engine is running on borrowed creator energy; whether Dobrá climbs in the broader Cafes family next issue depends almost entirely on whether its own channels start reflecting what 28 creators already see in it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: The City Has Seen 2.6 Million Plays Of This Tea House. The Tea House Has Seen None Of Them.

playbook.headline: Wake the Instagram account back up
playbook.action: With 18 days between posts and a 2.76% engagement rate still holding, a consistent two-posts-per-week cadence would immediately move the momentum signal off its current floor.

playbook.headline: Acknowledge the creators already filming here
playbook.action: Twenty-eight creators have posted 30-plus TikToks tagging this location — reposting even one per week on Instagram Stories converts existing reach into owned visibility without requiring original production.

playbook.headline: Expand the Google photo categories
playbook.action: 690 photos across 11 categories is a strong catalog, but adding staff-sourced images to underrepresented tags — particularly the board games, the table bell, and the snack platters reviewers keep naming — would deepen the visual argument for first-time visitors.
